prompt: Bug in our screen recorder: the recording timer in the UI displays 59 seconds when 60 seconds have actually elapsed. It's consistently 1 second behind throughout the recording. The relevant artifacts are in this fixture folder: spec at spec.md, plan at plan.md, implementation snippet at code.md, project constitution at ../../shared/constitution.md. Please debug as a staff engineer in our SDD workflow and produce a Triage Report.

ground_truth.rationale: Spec is correct (TREQ-001/002/003 cover the intended behavior). Plan is correct (TD-2's algorithm is right). Code has a stray '- 1' in the seconds computation in format(). Regen from current spec+plan would NOT reproduce this bug — an LLM regenerating the formatter from TD-2 would write the correct algorithm. This is a textbook implementation gap. Per constitution P6, fix lands with a regression test.

assertions.text: Layer Classification → Primary gap is 'implementation' (NOT 'spec' or 'plan').
Regeneration Test → Result is 'Pass' (current spec+plan would NOT reproduce the bug).
The diagnosis identifies the stray '- 1' in the format() function specifically.
The proposed fix is a code change, with a regression test (per Constitution P6).
The skill does NOT propose any spec amendment, plan amendment, or constitutional amendment for this case.
Constitutional Check → Implies project-wide invariant is 'no'.
Cited requirement IDs (e.g. TREQ-001) actually exist in spec.md.
